articles.title: Import Restriction Not Applicable to Goods Arrived Before Gazette Publication: Delhi HC
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court held that import restrictions could not apply to consignments that had arrived before the relevant notification was published in the Official Gazette. The petition, filed by the Centre for Consumer Education, Pala, seeks review of Government Order, which introduced the "Priyadarshini" scheme under the State Government's broader “Indira Guarantee” programme. articles.title: Is The Supreme Court Overstepping On Its Draft AI Regulations, 2026?
articles.summary: Whether Draft AI Rules Apply To High Courts ? On 3 June the Supreme Court released a draft for public comment, titled, “Regulations for Use of Artificial Intelligence in Courts, 2026.” The instinct is sound since litigants, lawyers and Court already use AI and guardrails are long overdue. But the fine print presents a constitutional problem. The issue is that it is just not a set of housekeeping rules for the Court's own registry or administration as you may expect under Article 145. It is a bin

articles.title: Can a Private Limited Company Accept a Loan From a Partnership Firm?
articles.summary: Private limited companies in India cannot accept loans from partnership firms without disclosing it as a deposit. This is according to the Companies (Acceptance of Deposits) Rules, 2014. The loan may be treated as a deposit even if partners are directors of the company.
articles.raw_summary: The article explains that loans received from partnership firms do not qualify as exempt borrowings under the Companies (Acceptance of Deposits) Rules, 2014. articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad held that isolated WhatsApp messages and electronic communications cannot, by themselves, support additions in search assessments. The Tribunal deleted several additions because no corroborative evidence established actual cash transactions. The ruling underscores that suspicion cannot replace proof in tax proceedings. The post WhatsApp Chats Alone Cannot Justify Tax Additions Without Supporting Evidence: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Deletes BMA Penalty as Foreign Assets Were Disclosed in Section 153A Returns
articles.summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad deleted a penalty under the Black Money Act as the taxpayer had disclosed foreign assets in their returns filed under Section 153A. This decision relied on the principle that such returns substitute the original returns. The taxpayer was exempt from the penalty.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that penalty under Section 43 of the Black Money Act could not be imposed when foreign assets were subsequently disclosed in returns filed under Section 153A. The Tribunal relied on the principle that such returns substitute the original returns. The post ITAT Deletes BMA Penalty as Foreign Assets Were Disclosed in Section 153A Returns appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Interest for failure to Collect/Deduct & for delayed TDS/TCS Payment

articles.summary: The Delhi ITAT held that reassessment proceedings were invalid where the Assessing Officer made additions unrelated to the reasons initially recorded for reopening. articles.summary: The Telangana High Court has ruled that transferring assessment proceedings under Section 127 of the Income Tax Act is valid to facilitate a coordinated investigation involving interconnected entities. articles.summary: India’s renewable energy journey has already achieved significant milestones. Yet the next stage of growth will require a broader perspective on infrastructure development.

articles.title: Maruti Suzuki shares jump over 4%. How is the new E100 regulation triggering a surge?
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki shares surged after the government approved legal recognition for 100% ethanol blend fuel (E100). This move, championed by Nitin Gadkari, aims to reduce India's import dependence and boost energy security. Maruti's recent launch of India's first flex-fuel passenger vehicle positions it as a direct beneficiary of this policy shift.

Maruti's recent launch of India's first flex-fuel passenger vehicle positions it as a direct beneficiary of this policy shift.

articles.title: PM-WANI reforms to enhance adoption of public Wi-Fi, boost internet accessibility: Experts
articles.summary: Recent government reforms to the PM-WANI framework, including short-duration data plans and QR-based authentication, are expected to boost entrepreneurship and affordable internet access. Experts believe these user-friendly measures will accelerate adoption, bridge the digital divide, and unlock new economic opportunities across India.

articles.title: India's trade with West Asia gradually improving: Commerce Secy
articles.summary: India's trade with West Asia faced disruptions in May due to the US-Iran conflict. Exports saw a slight dip but are recovering. Imports also declined significantly. The conflict impacted shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz. Despite challenges, Indian exporters are utilizing Omani ports. A peace deal between the US and Iran is anticipated to ease trade further.

A peace deal between the US and Iran is anticipated to ease trade further.

articles.title: India posts $4.7 bn current account surplus in April on strong remittances: RBI
articles.summary: India posted a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April, reversing a deficit a year ago, as stronger services exports and higher remittance inflows helped offset a wider merchandise trade gap.
articles.raw_summary: India posted a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April, reversing a deficit a year ago, as stronger services exports and higher remittance inflows helped offset a wider merchandise trade gap.
articles.context_summary: India achieved a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April 2026. This positive development occurred even with a larger merchandise trade deficit. Stronger net services exports and a significant rise in net transfers contributed to this surplus. Foreign direct investment also saw a substantial increase. However, foreign portfolio investment and banking capital experienced outflows.

articles.title: India's Russian oil imports rise in May as refiners boost purchases
articles.summary: India continued its strong demand for Russian fossil fuels in May. The nation emerged as the second-largest buyer globally, importing hydrocarbons worth 5.8 billion euros. Crude oil formed the bulk of these imports, with refiners significantly increasing their purchases from Russia. This trend highlights India's strategic energy sourcing amidst global shifts.
articles.raw_summary: India continued its strong demand for Russian fossil fuels in May. The nation emerged as the second-largest buyer globally, importing hydrocarbons worth 5.8 billion euros. Crude oil formed the bulk of these imports, with refiners significantly increasing their purchases from Russia. This trend highlights India's strategic energy sourcing amidst global shifts.

articles.title: Govt to replace WPI with Producer Price Index, new series from June 15
articles.summary: The Indian government will replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with the Producer Price Index (PPI) starting June 15. This change aims to better measure price changes at the producer level. The WPI is currently used to track average price changes in a basket of commodities. The shift to PPI reflects an update in economic indicators used for policy and analysis.
articles.raw_summary: WPI is a key indicator for measuring the average change in the prices of a fixed basket of commodities at the initial stage of a commercial transaction over a given period, with reference to a base year.

The court allowed an appeal filed by seven employees who had served for over a decade. This decision will impact the recruitment process in Haryana cooperative societies.

articles.title: Govt said to accept tweaks to Securities Markets Code draft
articles.summary: The government has accepted significant changes to the proposed Securities Markets Code. Investigation timelines will be extended to one year. Depositories will gain powers to correct records. The Centre's authority to supersede market infrastructure institution boards will now require Sebi's recommendation. These revisions follow stakeholder consultations and aim to strengthen market regulation.

articles.title: Rupee rallies to five-week high on crude oil plunge; RBI measures add to momentum
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened against the US dollar for a second consecutive day, buoyed by a significant drop in crude oil prices following a preliminary US-Iran agreement. This rise also builds on recent Reserve Bank of India measures aimed at attracting dollar inflows. Traders anticipate further support for the rupee, though its appreciation pace may hinge on the RBI's policy.

articles.summary: Indian and UK trade ministers held a virtual meeting. They discussed strengthening collaboration in innovation, technology, research, startups, and emerging technologies. This meeting is significant as a free trade agreement is in place. Certain issues have created hurdles in its implementation. The Indian minister is expected to visit London soon. Both ministers met on June 2.

articles.title: Remittances, services surplus lift India to $4.7 billion current account surplus in April
articles.summary: India achieved a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April. This positive shift was driven by increased inward remittances. The services sector also saw a surplus. However, the capital account turned deficit as foreign investors withdrew funds. Despite this, foreign direct investment saw a significant rise. Exporters have adapted to the global economic situation.

Exporters have adapted to the global economic situation.

articles.title: Unemployment rate hits 11-month high of 5.5% in May
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to a high in May. Rural joblessness saw a significant rise, reaching a 12-month peak. Urban unemployment eased slightly, particularly for women. Labour force participation and employment rates also saw a moderation. Youth unemployment edged up. These shifts suggest a softening in labour market conditions.

Urban unemployment eased slightly, particularly for women. Labour force participation and employment rates also saw a moderation. Youth unemployment edged up. These shifts suggest a softening in labour market conditions.

articles.title: India introduces PPI for goods and services, begins shift from WPI
articles.summary: India has launched Producer Price Index data for goods and services. This move aims to provide a clearer picture of price changes from a producer's viewpoint. The government plans to phase out Wholesale Price Index numbers within five years. This aligns with international practices and IMF recommendations. The new PPI will offer better insights into inflation experienced by producers.

The new PPI will offer better insights into inflation experienced by producers.

articles.title: Finalising NFL's tender to import 17 lakh tonnes of urea; stock comfortable for Kharif season: Government
articles.summary: India is securing vital fertilizer supplies for the ongoing Kharif season. The government is finalizing imports of 17 lakh tonnes of urea to bolster domestic stock. Overall fertilizer availability is comfortable, with current stocks significantly exceeding usual levels. Farmers have already purchased a substantial portion of their needs.

articles.title: India's unemployment rate rises to 5.5% in May
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate saw an increase in May, reaching 5.5%. This marks a rise from April's 5.2%. Government data indicates a slight uptick in joblessness. Both the Labour Force Participation Rate and Worker Population Ratio moderated. This suggests a softening of labour market conditions. Employment opportunities contracted, leading to more unemployed individuals within the labour force.

Employment opportunities contracted, leading to more unemployed individuals within the labour force.

articles.title: India’s goods exports up 18% to $45.2 bn in May
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports reached a record $45.2 billion in May. Imports also grew, leading to a trade deficit of $28.21 billion. Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal expressed optimism for FY27 if export momentum continues. The West Asia crisis impacted March exports, but recovery is noted. India's export base has expanded significantly over 12 years.

India's export base has expanded significantly over 12 years.

articles.title: USTR arrives June 22 for trade talks; Commerce Secretary says US-Iran deal should bring lasting peace
articles.summary: India's trade data for May 2026 shows robust export growth. Merchandise exports rose 18 percent year-on-year. Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal welcomed the US-Iran peace agreement. He expressed hope for regional stability. Discussions are ongoing for a trade agreement with the US. The US Trade Representative is expected to visit India soon to finalize the deal framework.

articles.title: India's trade deficit narrows slightly to $28.21 billion in May as petroleum exports cushion energy import blow
articles.summary: India's trade deficit saw a slight reduction in May. A recent US-Iran agreement is bringing welcome relief. This deal is expected to stabilize shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Consequently, global crude oil prices should moderate. This will ease pressure on India's import bill and help control inflation. Exporters anticipate a trade rebound with the Gulf region.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal brings relief, but India turns to a risk at home
articles.summary: India just got a near-term macroeconomic breather from a US-Iran peace deal. However, early monsoon weakness and potential El Niño conditions are emerging as the next major test for growth and price stability, shifting focus from imported energy stress to domestic climate risk.

articles.title: WPI inflation surges to all-time high of 9.7% under new series
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation hit a record 9.7% in May. This marks the first reading under a new Wholesale Price Index series. Fuel and power prices significantly drove this increase. Manufactured products and primary articles also saw higher inflation. The government also released new price indices for producers and services.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal may help boost India's exports, stabilise rupee
articles.summary: A peace deal between the US and Iran is set to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. This agreement is anticipated to significantly boost India's exports to West Asia. It will also help stabilize the Indian rupee and reduce inflationary pressures. The reopening of this vital shipping route promises economic relief and new business opportunities for India.

articles.title: Angel One settles Sebi proceedings over lapses in monitoring authorised persons, pays Rs 4.28 crore
articles.summary: Angel One has settled Sebi's adjudication and enquiry proceedings by paying Rs 4.28 crore. The regulator had alleged supervisory and monitoring lapses involving two authorised persons, including failures in due diligence, inspection processes, fund collection oversight and scrutiny of unauthorised activities.

articles.title: Waaree Energies gets shareholders' nod to raise up to Rs 10,000 cr via QIP
articles.summary: Waaree Energies shareholders have approved a significant capital raise. The company can now raise up to Rs 10,000 crore through a Qualified Institutions Placement. This move will bolster the company's financial standing. Additionally, Jignesh Devchandbhai Rathod has been appointed as Whole-Time Director and CEO. These approvals were confirmed through a remote e-voting process.

This residential endeavor boasts a massive 1.6 million square feet of developable space, underscoring Embassy Developments' role as a major player in India's vibrant real estate landscape.

articles.title: Madhusudan Kela buys luxury flat for Rs 121 cr in DLF's Gurugram project
articles.summary: Ace investor Madhusudan Murlidhar Kela has purchased a luxurious flat for Rs 121 crore in DLF's new project, 'The Dahlias', in Gurugram. The agreement was registered on April 15, 2026. DLF's project, launched in October 2024, has already seen significant sales, with around 60 percent of apartments sold. The project has a substantial revenue potential.

articles.title: Oil slide sends India's benchmark yield to two-month low
articles.summary: Indian government bonds saw a significant rally on Monday. The benchmark yield reached a two-month low. This surge followed news of a preliminary peace deal between the U.S. and Iran. Oil prices dropped sharply, benefiting India's finances. Foreign investors are showing renewed interest in Indian bonds. This positive sentiment is expected to continue.

articles.title: Anil Agarwal bets $20 billion on aluminium, steel, and zinc, and says Vedanta is only getting started
articles.summary: Vedanta Group Chairman Anil Agarwal announced a $20 billion capital expenditure plan over three years, aiming to triple group businesses. The ambitious expansion targets aluminium, steel, power, and zinc, driven by strong demand and raw material backing. Agarwal emphasized building for India's growth, not consolidating, with steel identified as a key new frontier.

articles.title: Advit Jewels announces price band for its IPO opening on June 23. Check details
articles.summary: Advit Jewels, a Jaipur-based jewellery maker, has announced its Initial Public Offering. The price band is set between Rs 130 and Rs 138 per share. The subscription opens on June 23 and closes on June 25. The company aims to raise approximately Rs 165 crore through this public issue. Funds will support working capital needs and business expansion.

articles.title: Nilesh Shah bats for minimum qualifying criteria for F&O trading after Maharashtra man kills family, self over Rs 1.8 cr loss
articles.summary: Following a tragic incident in Maharashtra linked to stock market losses, Kotak AMC's Nilesh Shah called for mandatory qualification criteria for derivatives trading. Citing rising retail F O losses and SEBI data showing most traders lose money, he urged stronger safeguards to curb excessive speculation in the market.

articles.title: Oil retreat hands RBI an assist in boosting rupee's near-term outlook
articles.summary: A U.S.-Iran peace deal has led to a slump in oil prices, bolstering the Indian rupee. This, combined with the Reserve Bank of India's measures to attract foreign inflows, is expected to support the currency's near-term trajectory. Analysts anticipate the rupee to appreciate as import bills decrease and investor confidence returns.

articles.title: UP man arrested in Nepal with drugs concealed in undergarments

articles.title: India, UK work to resolve issues holding up trade pact implementation
articles.summary: India and the UK are actively engaged in resolving outstanding issues to operationalize their free trade agreement, signed in July last year. Discussions are ongoing to find a resolution.

articles.title: India launches producer price index; wholesale inflation gauge to be phased out in five years
articles.summary: India has launched its first Producer Price Index (PPI) data for goods and services, marking a shift from the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) which will be phased out over five years. This move aligns with international practices and aims to provide a more accurate measure of price changes from a producer's perspective, aiding in National Accounts compilation.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has clarified the law on using DNA tests to determine paternity. The court ruled that an individual's right to privacy does not override the need to ascertain biological parentage. This decision will impact individuals seeking to establish paternity and has significant implications for family law in India.

articles.title: ‘50-year demand’ nears fulfilment as Zojila tunnel breakthrough brings Ladakh closer to all-weather connectivity
articles.summary: The Zojila tunnel, a key infrastructure project in Ladakh, has achieved a breakthrough after a 50-year demand for all-weather connectivity. The tunnel, connecting Srinagar to Leh, is being developed by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) under the Ministry of Defence. This project aims to improve year-round access to Ladakh, which is currently cut off during winter due to heavy snowfall. The completion of the tunnel is expected to boost regional connectivity and strategic mobility.

articles.title: Chandigarh-Mohali-Panchkula cab strike: Ola, Uber services hit as drivers launch protest till June 16
articles.summary: Ola and Uber cab services in the Chandigarh-Mohali-Panchkula region were disrupted as drivers launched a strike protesting against their working conditions and demands for policy changes. The protest, which began on June 10, is set to continue until June 16. The drivers' demands include better earnings, fair working conditions, and regulatory protections for gig workers. The strike highlights ongoing concerns about the rights and welfare of platform-based workers in India.

articles.title: Odisha SHG Sends 7.5 Quintals of Amrapali Mangoes to London, Showcasing Odisha’s Global Agri Reach
articles.summary: An Angul-based women’s self-help group (SHG), Maa Kalijai, exported 7.5 quintals of Amrapali mangoes to London. This showcases Odisha’s growing agricultural export capabilities and the role of grassroots women’s groups in global markets. The initiative reflects broader trends in women’s empowerment and agri-business expansion in India.
articles.raw_summary: An Angul-based women’s SHG, Maa Kalijai, exported 7.5 quintals of Amrapali mangoes to London, showcasing Odisha’s growing agri-export success, women’s empowerment, and grassroots participation in global agricultural markets.

articles.title: Basmati rice, tea exports to West Asia come to a grinding halt
articles.summary: Indian basmati rice and tea exports to West Asia have stopped due to the US-Iran conflict. Vessels carrying rice are waiting at Kandla Port, while tea exporters face unfulfilled orders from GCC countries. This situation has created significant tension and pressure on exporter profitability.

articles.title: Farm credit demand likely to stay strong in FY27 on higher input costs, agri investments: NABARD
articles.summary: Farm credit demand is expected to remain strong in FY27 due to rising input costs and increased investments in agriculture. This is driven by factors such as expanded Kisan Credit Card coverage and higher investments in allied activities and infrastructure. The demand is expected to be strong, but higher borrowing costs pose a challenge for farmers and rural MSMEs.

articles.title: India's FTAs set stage for $1 trillion export target: Report
articles.summary: India's new generation of Free Trade Agreements can act as a catalyst for manufacturing expansion, private capex revival and supply-chain integration, with Electronics, Pharmaceuticals and Engineering   Machinery Goods positioned as the strongest beneficiaries. While this could alleviate water scarcity, it risks diminishing farm income and curtailing rural consumer activity.

articles.title: El Nino sets in, clouds kharif outlook
articles.summary: El Nino conditions have set in and may strengthen during the southwest monsoon, raising concerns for India's largest sowing window. articles.summary: India is expected to remain the world’s fastest-growing major economy, with the World Bank projecting GDP growth of 6.6% in FY27, down from 7.7% in FY26 due to higher energy prices and input costs affecting private demand. Growth is forecast to recover to 7.2% in FY28, aided by stronger domestic demand, export growth, lower US tariffs and free trade agreements.

articles.title: Ujjwala Yojana: Subsidised LPG cylinders capped at 4
articles.summary: The government has reduced the number of subsidized LPG cylinders under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) from nine to four per year for beneficiaries. PMUY, launched in 2016, provides free LPG connections to adult women from poor households. This change impacts millions of households relying on subsidized fuel for cooking. The decision follows earlier reductions from 12 to nine cylinders last year.
articles.raw_summary: Under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY), launched in May 2016 to provide deposit-free LPG connections to adult women from poor households, beneficiaries were initially entitled to 12 subsidised 14.2-kg cylinders a year. The subsidised quota was reduced to nine cylinders last year and has now been cut further to four.

articles.title: Markets Espresso: India's time will come and there are pockets of opportunities
